landing.subtitle: Install the app, pick a local vault folder, and start. Map features, modules, and dependencies as markdown files; browse them as a tree or graph; let AI agents work on the same files.
landing.topologyCaption: TOPOLOGY · {nodes, plural, one {# PROJECT} other {# PROJECTS}} · {relations, plural, one {# RELATION} other {# RELATIONS}}
landing.step1Title: Write a markdown file per piece
landing.step1Body: One file per project / feature / module. Add a name and a few links in the header — that's a node, those are relations. AI agents (Claude Code, etc.) read and edit the same files.
landing.step2Body: "Who depends on this feature?" "What breaks if I change it?" — the answer is one click away instead of a search across files.
landing.step3Body: Browse as a tree, navigate as a graph, edit on a canvas. Distribution charts, dependency chains, hub nodes — all from the same folder.

openSource.title: Built in public for developers who use AI coding agents.
openSource.body: Context Atlas is the user-facing app for the MIT-licensed oh-my-ontology project by stark (Jinan Choi / 최진안). It exists because AI coding agents keep forgetting the long-term shape of a codebase. The project keeps that shape in markdown frontmatter, inside your repo, so Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, and humans can share one memory.
openSource.stackValue: Next.js · TypeScript · Sigma.js · MCP · static hosting

landing.downloadNote: Public builds ship through the latest GitHub Release after signing, notarization, checksum verification, and download-asset checks pass.
landing.openVaultCta: Open local vault in the app
landing.privacyNote: The app and vault data use your local disk as the source of truth. The website is the product introduction and download entry point.

download.title: Install once. Work from your local vault.
download.subtitle: The desktop app opens your markdown vault directly, remembers recent vaults, and keeps the hosted site out of your daily ontology workflow.
download.releaseAvailabilityNote: If no macOS DMG is visible yet, the app release is still waiting on PR review, version alignment, Apple signing, or the v0.1.0 GitHub Release.
download.releaseStatusTitle: Before the first release is fully available
download.releaseStatusPr: PR #274 must be reviewed and merged before v0.1.0 can ship.
download.releaseStatusVersion: The v0.1.0 tag must match package.json, Tauri, and Cargo metadata.
download.releaseStatusSecrets: Apple Developer ID signing/notarization secrets must be configured before the macOS app release.
download.releaseStatusRelease: The v0.1.0 GitHub Release is the source of truth for verified Apple Silicon and Intel DMGs.
download.releaseStatusHosted: Separately, Firebase Hosting must deploy the promo/download site so /ko/download/ is live after the app release.
download.proofSigned: Release gate requires Developer ID signing
download.proofChecksum: Apple Silicon + Intel checksums are verified
download.step1Body: Use the latest stable GitHub Release. Choose the aarch64 DMG for Apple Silicon Macs, or the x64 DMG for Intel Macs. Public builds are published only after signing, notarization, and checksum verification.
download.step2Body: Open the DMG, copy the app into Applications, then launch Context Atlas like any local macOS app.
download.step3Body: The app starts with a vault setup screen, then opens the native folder picker when you choose a local folder. Recent local vaults reopen without returning to the website.
download.releaseGateNote: The hosted site does not open or edit vault folders. Daily ontology work starts inside the installed app after the release asset passes the macOS signing, notarization, and checksum gates.

sigma.pathStartBody: Click a source node, then click a target to highlight the shortest visible route. Shift+click also works outside Path mode.

page.title: Concept Save/edit
page.helpIntro: A workspace where you draw your knowledge graph by hand.
page.helpStepPalette: Click a kind from the palette on the left to add a new concept.
page.helpStepConnect: Drag a concept handle and drop it on another concept. Vault↔vault relations show a write preview first; choose the relation key, then save.
page.helpStepEphemeral: Draft concepts and relations use dashed styling — name the concept in the details panel, then click Save on the edge to commit both at once.
page.helpShortcuts: P · project / D · domain / C · capability / E · element  ·  Del · delete selected  ·  Esc · clear  ·  F · fullscreen

writeSummary.summarySubtitle: Open only when you need save connection, draft safety, relation guard, or graph proof.

page.mobileTitle: Save/edit works best on a bigger screen
page.mobileBody: This screen moves between the palette, canvas, and detail dialogs for precise relation repair, so mobile input is too cramped. Open it again on desktop or tablet landscape. Meanwhile you can browse or validate the ontology from here.

page.toastDemoModePicker: Demo mode — open a local vault folder to save ontology files.
page.toastVaultConflict: This vault file was changed first by another editor or AI agent. Refresh and save again.

page.toastVaultEdgeDemoPicker: Demo mode — open a local vault folder to add relations.

relationPostSave.title: Relation saved
relationPostSave.body: Keep the graph context alive: inspect the saved edge in Topology Path mode, check either endpoint in Focus mode, then hand the sync gate to the next Claude Code or Codex run.
relationPostSave.queryCockpitAriaLabel: Verify target concept in query cockpit: {target}

relationConfirm.title: Confirm relation write
relationConfirm.body: Save/edit inferred `{inferredKey}` from {sourceKind} → {targetKind}. Review the meaning before changing frontmatter.
relationConfirm.writeBoundaryValue: source frontmatter only; target file remains unchanged
relationConfirm.mcpWritePolicyReady: Direct MCP write payload is enabled only after the read checks are clear and the selected key still matches the intended graph meaning.
relationConfirm.mcpWritePolicyBlocked: Direct MCP write payload stays disabled until relation_check, path, and bounded all_paths evidence are reviewed.
relationConfirm.graphAlternativeWarning: You selected a different key than Save/edit inferred. Save only if this relation is the clearer graph meaning.
relationConfirm.endpointReviewBody: Open both endpoints before saving when the concept meaning or canvas placement is uncertain.
relationConfirm.postSaveGraphHandoffBody: After saving, open the new edge in Topology Path mode or inspect either endpoint in Focus mode before running the graph DB proof gate.
relationConfirm.preflightActionSafe: Preflight is clear. Save only after the selected key and frontmatter patch match the graph meaning you want.
relationConfirm.preflightActionReview: Preflight found existing graph context. Keep the review packet copyable, but run the read checks before committing this direct edge.
relationConfirm.preflightActionBlocked: This exact relation already exists. No frontmatter write is needed.
relationConfirm.traversalCheckBody: Run bounded all_paths before treating a shortest path or existing path as complete graph evidence.
relationConfirm.traversalContractBody: Report limit, searchBudget, expandedStates, exhaustive, truncatedByBudget, totalPathsExact, evidence.status, evidence.reason, and evidence.pathsComplete before using paths as write evidence.

safeToAdd.label: Safe to add
safeToAdd.hint: No duplicate, reverse edge, or existing path explains this relation yet.

skipExisting.label: Already exists
skipExisting.hint: This exact source-side frontmatter entry already exists, so saving is disabled.

reviewInverse.label: Review inverse direction
reviewInverse.hint: The target already points back to the source. Confirm the direction before adding another edge.

reviewPath.label: Review existing path
reviewPath.hint: These nodes are already connected through the graph. Add this direct edge only if it carries a clearer meaning.

blastRadius.titleWithBacklinks: {count} nodes depend on this — delete anyway?
blastRadius.bodyClean: The node and its frontmatter will be removed from the vault. This cannot be undone.
blastRadius.bodyWithBacklinks: These nodes reference the one you're about to delete. Their links will become dangling — you can fix them after, but plan a cleanup pass.
blastRadius.hintClean: Tip: Save/edit writes straight to disk. Re-create the node any time.
blastRadius.hintWithBacklinks: Tip: open the listed nodes after delete to remove the dangling reference, or rename instead of deleting.

canvas.ephemeralEdgeSaveAria: Save this relation to the vault
canvas.ephemeralEdgeSaveTooltip: Save this relation. Unsaved nodes are saved first.
canvas.emptyHint: Click a kind in the left palette to create your first node.

inspector.subtitle: Edit the selected concept's name, document, and relations.
inspector.emptyHint: Click a kind in the left palette to create your first concept, or click any canvas concept to see its details here.
inspector.saveButtonAriaLabel: Save this concept to the local vault as a .md file
inspector.copySavePathAriaLabel: Copy file path to be saved: {path}
inspector.copyDraftAgentPacketAriaLabel: Copy draft concept packet for an AI agent: {path}
inspector.ephemeralSaveConflict: {path} already exists. Rename this draft to save a new concept.
inspector.ephemeralFooterEmpty: Enter a name to save. (Press Enter to save instantly)
inspector.ephemeralFooterConflict: A file with this path already exists. Rename before saving.
inspector.ephemeralFooterReady: Press Enter to save — written as a markdown (.md) file.
inspector.vaultFooterReadOnlyDownload: Sample data — install the macOS app to edit this graph in a local vault.
inspector.vaultFooterReadOnlyPicker: Sample data — click [Demo →] above to open a local vault folder.
inspector.vaultFooterEditable: Name saves with the button. Description and domain save after editing, and relation changes write to the same .md file in the local vault.
inspector.deleteAriaLabel: Delete this concept from the local vault
inspector.literalAutoSaveDirty: Unsaved draft — commits when you leave the field.
inspector.literalAutoSaveClean: Saving with an empty value removes the field entirely.
inspector.sourceDocumentHint: The body and frontmatter fields live in the same markdown file, so people and AI-agent MCP reads stay attached to the same local-vault file.
inspector.backlinksEmpty: No saved concepts reference this concept yet.

onboarding.title: Map your codebase together
onboarding.intro: A visual map of *features, relationships, and roles*. Draw it on the canvas — your AI agent (Claude Code, Cursor, …) reads and writes the same `.md` over MCP.
onboarding.stepPaletteStrong: Click a kind on the left (or press P/D/C/E)
onboarding.stepPaletteBody: — Project · Domain · Capability · Element. Hit the matching key to drop one without leaving the canvas.
onboarding.stepConnectStrong: Drag from a node's edge handle
onboarding.stepConnectBody: and drop on another node — the two get a *relation (depends / contains / describes)* between them. Vault↔vault relations open a write preview and preflight first; choose the relation key, then save. If either endpoint is unsaved (amber), click the **Save** chip on the edge to persist both at once.
onboarding.stepSaveBodyDownload: — straight to disk as `.md` when a vault is open. In the hosted browser this canvas is read-only; install the macOS app for writable local work. AI agents read and write the same map over MCP.
onboarding.stepSaveBodyPicker: — straight to disk as `.md` when a vault is open. Without a vault the canvas is read-only; open one from the top-right demo badge. AI agents read and write the same map over MCP.
onboarding.shortcutsHint: Quick keys — P/D/C/E new node · Del delete · Esc cancel

palette.subtitle: Pick a kind and click — a new node lands in the middle of the canvas.
palette.footerHint: Draft node → name in the details panel + Enter to save.
palette.kindProjectHint: Top-level — one project in the whole workspace
palette.kindDomainHint: A big area inside a project — auth / billing / notifications, …
palette.kindCapabilityHint: One feature inside a domain — verb-style like "sign in"
palette.kindElementHint: A small piece inside a capability — screen / button / API

insights.title: Insights
insights.subtitle: A query surface for validating the local markdown graph through scans, paths, health, and handoff. The tree shows hierarchy; insights show evidence contracts and the next graph action.
insights.emptyTitleBefore: Your ontology is empty. Start from the
insights.queryCockpitTitle: Run the local graph like a small database
insights.queryCockpitBody: Start with the bounded MATCH-style query pack before interpreting hubs, coupling, or paths. It keeps node scans, edge scans, domain matrix, and path evidence tied to the same markdown vault.
insights.queryCockpitNextStepBody: Copy the CLI pack for terminal fallback, then run the runtime gate before treating scan rows as evidence.
insights.queryCockpitCompactSummary: Readiness {readiness} · Pack {pack} scans · MCP {mcp} calls · CLI {cli} fallbacks · Runtime gate {runtime} checks
insights.queryCockpitLiveGraphBody: Graph facts computed from the current graph manifest right now.
insights.queryCockpitLiveHealthBody: {unknown} unresolved stubs · {orphans} orphans. Zero blockers means scan rows are ready to narrow.
insights.queryCockpitLiveTraversalBody: Hubs and average degree make path, blast radius, and domain matrix queries useful.
insights.queryCockpitEvidencePlanBody: Run query_plan first for costly scans and traversal so cost and bounds are explicit.
insights.queryCockpitEvidenceScanBody: Run match_nodes, match_edges, domain_matrix, and path queries over the same local markdown graph.
insights.queryCockpitEvidenceFollowUpBody: Scan rows are candidates. Narrow them with node_profile, match_edges, blast_radius, and explain_relation.
insights.queryCockpitEvidenceProofBody: Close the evidence before writing with relation_check, all_paths evidence, and the sync gate.
insights.queryCockpitScanContractBody: Before using match_nodes or match_edges as evidence, report totalMatches, limited, row count, and followUp.
insights.queryCockpitPathContractBody: For all_paths, report limit, searchBudget, expandedStates, exhaustive, totalPathsExact, and evidence.pathsComplete.
insights.queryCockpitProofBody: This JSON gate proves connectivity and fallback performance before scan commands run. Automation reads ok, performanceOk, failure count, and per-command timeout flags separately.
insights.queryCockpitRuntimeBody: The dogfood runtime gate then replays the same graph DB pack over the local markdown vault: health --json, focused blast_radius, scan follow-ups, relation_name_parity, pattern_walk/project_map containment, all_paths completeness, relation_check recommendation, and health check status all fail closed if their contract disappears.
insights.focusedProofBody: {kind} · {slug}. Save/edit and tree handoffs land here with node_profile, planned edge scans, relation parity, bounded path planning, relation_check, and the sync gate ready to copy.
insights.focusedProofSyncBody: {count} graph checks · health · cycles · growth_plan · maintenance_plan · validate_vault
insights.agentTerminalFallbackSubtitle: If the MCP connector is not registered yet, run these local CLI checks against the same vault.

agentNextAction.resolveUnknown: Resolve unknown/stub nodes with get_concept, find_evidence, then patch_concept or merge_concepts.
agentNextAction.addConcepts: Bootstrap the shape: add at least one domain, capability, and element before expecting useful graph answers.
agentNextAction.linkOrphans: Attach unconnected nodes with contains/domain/capabilities/elements relations so traversal has a spine.
agentNextAction.addRelations: Add real depends_on/relates/describes edges; sparse graphs make path and impact queries weak.
agentNextAction.inspectHubs: Start with workspace_brief, node_profile, and blast_radius on the suggested hub slugs.
agentNextAction.syncAfterChanges: After code changes, sync docs/ontology before finishing so Claude Code and Codex see the same graph.

insights.collaboratorInsightsSubtitle: A copyable overview for planning, marketing, and domain review that keeps shared vocabulary tied to the same local ontology graph.
insights.collaboratorVocabularyReuseAction: align naming, owner, and reuse context before external handoff
insights.collaboratorFocusAlignVocabulary: Align naming around the top hubs before teams reuse this vocabulary in plans, docs, or campaigns.
insights.collaboratorFocusTraceImpact: Trace cross-domain impact before changing scope, messaging, ownership, or implementation boundaries.
insights.collaboratorFocusResolveOrphans: Resolve open ownership questions first; unconnected concepts are not ready to become shared team language.
insights.collaboratorDecisionAlignExpected: Approve reused terms, rename unclear hubs, and confirm the owner for shared vocabulary.
insights.collaboratorDecisionAlignNextStep: Open hub handoffs in Ontology, Topology, or Save/edit before copying terms into plans or campaigns.
insights.collaboratorDecisionImpactExpected: Confirm affected domains, messaging promises, and scope boundaries before changing vocabulary.
insights.collaboratorDecisionImpactNextStep: Open the Topology Path handoff and replay the domain-matrix CLI/MCP checks.
insights.collaboratorDecisionOrphanOwner: Domain owner for each open concept
insights.collaboratorDecisionOrphanExpected: Assign a container, merge a duplicate, rename unclear language, or delete the orphan.
insights.collaboratorDecisionOrphanNextStep: Open the Save/edit or Topology health handoff, then run the agent ownership checks before writing.
insights.collaboratorQuestionAlignReuse: Which terms should planning, docs, or campaigns reuse exactly?
insights.collaboratorQuestionAlignRename: Which high-signal term needs a clearer name before handoff?
insights.collaboratorQuestionAlignOwner: Which shared vocabulary hub needs an explicit owner?
insights.collaboratorQuestionImpactDomains: Which domains are affected if this vocabulary or scope changes?
insights.collaboratorQuestionImpactMessaging: Which message, ownership, or roadmap promise needs review?
insights.collaboratorQuestionImpactBoundary: Which implementation boundary should not move with the wording?
insights.collaboratorQuestionOrphanContainer: Which domain, capability, or project should contain it?
insights.collaboratorQuestionOrphanAction: Should the concept be linked, merged, renamed, or deleted?
insights.agentRecipesSubtitle: First MCP graph calls to run from Claude Code or Codex, with exact JSON arguments.
insights.agentGraphDbPackSubtitle: Copy graph-database-style scans that keep local markdown evidence bounded, planned, and follow-up driven.
insights.agentGraphDbSelfCheckHint: The CLI pack starts with this JSON gate to verify connection and fallback timing, and the dogfood runtime passes the health gate before scan commands run.
insights.agentGraphDbModeCliDesc: Use validation, summaries, graph scans, paths, and graph DB packs from Terminal without MCP.
insights.agentGraphDbModeMcpDesc: Let Claude Code, Codex, or Cursor call local tools with structured repair fields and write guardrails.
insights.agentGraphDbModePackDesc: Explore with planned node/edge scans, domain matrix, paths, and relation explanations without a DB server.
insights.agentGraphDbModeGateDesc: Run the JSON fallback check before edits and read ok separately from performanceOk.
insights.agentGraphDbFacetsPrompt: Read kind, domain, degree, relation, and schema-pattern buckets before choosing a narrower graph query.
insights.agentGraphDbNodeScanPrompt: Find high-degree capabilities before onboarding, slicing work, or choosing a refactor entrypoint.
insights.agentGraphDbEdgeScanPrompt: Inspect dependency edges with query_plan first, then follow edge evidence before changing boundaries.
insights.agentGraphDbDomainCouplingPrompt: Read coupling and hub ranking together so domain-level architecture work starts from measured pressure.
insights.agentGraphDbPathEvidencePrompt: Plan and bound path enumeration, then explain the relation before treating a path as proof.
insights.agentGraphDbPackStepsLabel: Graph DB query pack MCP calls
insights.agentHandoffSubtitle: Copy this into a fresh Claude Code or Codex session to make ontology reads, health gates, query planning, and vault sync the default workflow.
insights.agentTraversalContractBudget: Report limit, searchBudget, expandedStates, exhaustive, truncatedByBudget, totalPathsExact, evidence.status, evidence.reason, and evidence.pathsComplete.
insights.agentTraversalContractPartial: Treat paths as partial evidence unless evidence.pathsComplete is true; when evidence.status is partial, follow suggestedQuery or saferQuery.
insights.agentTraversalContractProof: Do not use one returned path as proof when the query is limited, budget-truncated, or pathsComplete is false.
insights.agentScanContractTotals: Report totalMatches, limited, row count, and followUp details before treating scan rows as evidence.
insights.agentScanContractNodeFollowUp: For match_nodes, run followUp calls such as node_profile, match_edges, and blast_radius before onboarding or refactor decisions.
insights.agentScanContractEdgeFollowUp: For match_edges, run followUp calls such as explain_relation, path, and relation_check before write, refactor, or coupling evidence.
insights.agentTraversalStrategySubtitle: Plan before enumerating paths, treat bounded all_paths as evidence, then cross-check project/domain containment.
insights.agentTraversalStrategyPlanPrompt: Run query_plan(all_paths) first so high-degree hubs and broad bounds are narrowed before traversal.
insights.agentTraversalStrategyBoundedPrompt: Run all_paths only with maxHops, relation types, limit, and searchBudget, then report evidence completeness.
insights.agentTraversalStrategyContainmentPrompt: Compare pattern_walk and project_map before changing ownership, domain boundaries, or relation direction.
insights.agentRunOrderSubtitle: A compact first-contact sequence with MCP calls and CLI fallback for agent sessions.
insights.agentEntrypointsSubtitle: Use these high-degree nodes when a graph query needs a concrete slug instead of a placeholder.
insights.agentPlaybooksSubtitle: Intent-first graph workflows that bundle several MCP calls for common agent tasks.
insights.agentPlaybookRefactorPrompt: Profile a target slug, plan cost, inspect incoming blast radius and path evidence, then preflight relation changes.
insights.agentPlaybookOnboardingPrompt: Build a first mental map from workspace health, domain coupling, and one concrete hub profile.
insights.agentPlaybookCouplingPrompt: Check health, domain coupling, central nodes, and dependency edges before changing boundaries.
insights.agentPlaybookTraversalPrompt: Use schema, all_paths, pattern_walk, and project_map when an answer needs graph-query evidence instead of a single route.
insights.agentGuardrailsSubtitle: Read-first MCP preflights for the vault writes that usually cause stale edges, duplicate relations, or broken backlinks.
insights.agentRelationDecisionSubtitle: Use the decision field before any add_relation write. This keeps agent mutations explainable instead of raw graph edits.
insights.agentRelationDecisionSkipExisting: Exact edge already exists. Do not add another copy.
insights.agentRelationDecisionReviewInverse: Reverse edge exists. Check direction before writing.
insights.agentRelationDecisionSafeToAdd: Schema pattern is familiar. Add only after path evidence still supports it.
insights.agentRelationDecisionReviewNewSchema: New schema pattern. Pause and explain why this relation type belongs here.
insights.agentGuardrailRelationPrompt: Run relation_check and path first, then follow recommendation.decision so the edge is not duplicated, inverted, or already explained by a better relation.
insights.agentGuardrailRenamePrompt: Inspect backlinks and the local node profile before rewriting slugs that other concepts cite.
insights.agentGuardrailSyncPrompt: Gate the resulting graph with the 14-check runtime graph DB pack, health, cycles, growth_plan, maintenance_plan, and validate_vault before handing work back to another agent.
insights.agentRecipePromptAgentBrief: Start here when another agent needs the graph state, entrypoints, playbooks, and read-first write policy in one response.
insights.agentRecipePromptWorkspaceBrief: Start here for status, hotspots, health checks, and next actions.
insights.agentRecipePromptQueryPlan: Ask for cost, limits, and indexes before running a heavier graph query.
insights.agentRecipePromptHealth: Gate the vault before writes: unresolved refs, cycles, components, recommendations.
insights.agentRecipePromptComponents: Find disconnected graph islands before trusting traversal or onboarding maps.
insights.agentRecipePromptCycles: Check directed depends_on cycles and use the next cycle evidence loop before repair.
insights.agentRecipePromptTopologicalOrder: Read prerequisite-first dependency layers before sequencing refactors or onboarding.
insights.agentRecipePromptGrowthPlan: Review growth candidates and preflight recommended relations before writing to the vault.
insights.agentRecipePromptMaintenancePlan: Open the ordered maintenance queue and narrow by phase, severity, or kind before acting.
insights.agentRecipePromptNodeProfile: Inspect one hub deeply: aliases, incoming/outgoing edges, containment, lineage, and local risk.
insights.agentRecipePromptPath: Prove how two important nodes are connected before changing a boundary or relation.
insights.agentRecipePromptExplainRelation: Explain why two nodes are related with direct edges, shortest path, domain context, and common neighbors.
insights.agentRecipePromptSimilarNodes: Before creating or renaming a concept, check likely duplicates and shared-neighbor signals.
insights.agentRecipePromptRelationCheck: Preflight a proposed add_relation: inspect recommendation.decision, inverseEdges, schema familiarity, and the exact add_relation args to use.
insights.agentRecipePromptBlastRadius: Use before refactors to inspect incoming impact and dependency risk for a slug.
insights.agentRecipePromptDomainMatrix: Compare domain coupling and find where the ontology is becoming too tangled.
insights.agentRecipePromptAllPaths: Enumerate bounded simple paths between two important slugs before deciding which relationship actually explains the connection.
insights.agentRecipePromptPatternWalk: Run an explicit relation-sequence traversal such as project → domains → capabilities when hierarchy matters.
insights.bandProofDesc: Whether this graph is ready for an AI agent to traverse, and the exact MCP / CLI calls to verify and query it.
insights.bandCensusDesc: Structural distribution by the numbers: node kinds, relation types, and domain coupling. Describes the vault, not its agent-readiness.
insights.projectPanelSubtitle: {count} projects total — domain · capability · element sums, sorted by size
insights.edgeTypePanelSubtitle: {count} relations total — split by semantic relation type
insights.domainCouplingReproduce: Re-run this semantic matrix from Claude Code, Codex, or a terminal with the same relation filters.
insights.domainCouplingEmpty: No cross-domain links yet. Domain boundaries are clean in the current graph.
insights.hubsEmpty: No connected nodes yet. Link nodes in Save/edit and the most-connected ones (hubs) will surface here.
insights.orphansPanelSubtitle: {count} nodes not hanging from the tree
insights.orphansEmpty: Every node is connected to the tree.
insights.orphansRepairPacketInspectNode: Inspect the node in topology health mode.
insights.orphansRepairPacketChooseOwner: Choose the nearest domain, capability, or project owner.
insights.orphansRepairPacketSyncGate: Run the post-change sync gate before handing the graph to another agent.

selector.emptyDescCanCreate: Create your first project to get going.
selector.quickCreateSubmit: Create and write the first doc
selector.newProjectDesc: Quickly add a new project from the bottom of the list and jump to its first doc.
selector.cardDescriptionFallback: This project doesn't have a description yet.

detail.stateInvalidDesc: No valid project slug, so detail can't be displayed.
detail.inlineDescriptionPlaceholder: Describe this project in one line
detail.depCardDescription: Pick the hubs and services this project relies on; the topology updates instantly. Cycle-causing candidates are disabled.
detail.neighborMapDescription: A small map containing only the projects directly linked to this one. Drag, hover, and click behave just like the main topology.
detail.neighborMapEmpty: No directly linked projects yet. Add a slug under `dependencies:` in this project's frontmatter and the neighbors will appear here.
detail.infoCardDescription: Project description written by the workspace owner.
detail.neighborsCardDescription: Other projects directly depended on or referenced.
detail.neighborsMoreNote: You can browse all {count} connections in the connection map section of the main content.
detail.neighborsEmpty: No links to other projects yet. Add a project slug to `dependencies:` in the frontmatter and it'll show up here.
detail.saveErrorConflict: This project file was changed first by another editor or AI agent. Refresh and save again.

editor.confirmDiscardChanges: You have unsaved changes. Leave anyway?
editor.loadErrorDuplicate: The project to duplicate could not be found.
editor.vaultConflict: This project file was changed first by another editor or AI agent. Refresh and save again.
editor.createdAndOpenToast: "{name}" created · fill in details on the edit screen
editor.headerSubtitle: An edit screen that organizes metadata, connections, screenshots, and detailed descriptions in one place.
editor.openPublicAria: Open the public view in a new tab
editor.savedNotice: Just saved. You can keep refining here, or open the public view to verify the result immediately.
editor.tipTitleEasiest: Fill in name, category, status, and a short description, then save first.
editor.tipDescEasiest: Reopen later to enrich connections, screenshots, and operational info gradually.
editor.tipFirstTimeStep1: 1. The slug auto-fills as you type the name.
editor.tipFirstTimeStep2: 2. A single short description fills out the public card and drawer.
editor.tipFirstTimeStep3: 3. Detail fields can be expanded below and filled in as needed.
editor.publicCompanionDesc: Even while editing, you can open the public detail and linked docs to see the actual published state. Changes appear immediately on the public view after saving and reopening.

header.title: Source Vault
header.topologyTooltip: Open the graph view for this vault

vaultStatus.localTooltip: Pick a folder on your disk as the vault
vaultStatus.desktopOnlyTooltip: Local vault work now starts in the installed macOS app. Use the download page to install it.
vaultStatus.unsupportedTooltip: Local vault editing is available in the installed macOS app.
vaultStatus.permissionNeededBanner: Vault is paused — re-grant folder access to keep editing. Showing sample docs until then.
vaultStatus.errorBanner: Vault folder is unavailable ({message}). Showing sample docs — open the picker to re-pick or refresh.

desktopWelcome.title: Open or create an ontology vault
desktopWelcome.body: Choose a markdown folder to use as your vault. Context Atlas keeps the files on this Mac, remembers recent vaults, and turns frontmatter into the graph.
desktopWelcome.contractFilesBody: Your `.md` files remain in the folder you choose. Git can review every graph change.
desktopWelcome.contractGraphBody: Kinds and relation keys compile into browse, topology, save/edit, and query views.
desktopWelcome.contractAgentBody: Claude Code, Codex, and Cursor use the same local graph DB proof gate before writes.
desktopWelcome.openBody: Select a folder that already contains markdown ontology files.
desktopWelcome.createBody: Make or choose an empty folder, then seed it with the starter after it opens.
desktopWelcome.sampleBody: Look around with read-only docs before choosing a local folder.
desktopWelcome.recentEmpty: No recent vaults yet. Open or create a folder to pin it here.

sourceContract.filesBody: The selected folder is the source of truth; every ontology change is a frontmatter diff.
sourceContract.graphBody: Frontmatter compiles into browse, topology, save/edit, and query surfaces over the same vault.
sourceContract.agentBody: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and terminal sessions run the same graph DB proof gate before writes, including relation_name_parity and pattern_walk/project_map containment replay.
sourceContract.agentCopyGateAriaLabel: Copy the source vault graph DB runtime gate

advanced.ontologyHintPrefix: First time? Try selecting this repo's
advanced.ontologyHintSuffix: — this tool's own ontology vault appears in the tree instantly (dogfooding).

vaultReveal.errorFallback: Could not open the vault in Finder.

agentSetup.title: AI agent setup
agentSetup.rootSummaryReady: This vault's .mcp.json / .codex are ready with OMOT_VAULT=.
agentSetup.rootSummaryMissing: Vault-folder setup · separate codebase roots need an absolute-path connection
agentSetup.connectionHint: Ready means the config files are present. Context Atlas does not connect to the agent directly, so restart the agent and verify the live MCP connection inside that agent.
agentSetup.boundaryDesc: Context Atlas does not open Claude Code, Codex, or Cursor chat inside the app. It prepares local MCP config, restart guidance, and verification gates so each agent can read and write this vault from its own app or terminal.
agentSetup.nextInvalid: Review: {path} is not an oh-my-ontology MCP config
agentSetup.stepConfigs: Create or review the MCP and Codex config files in this vault.
agentSetup.stepRestart: Restart Claude Code, Cursor, or Codex from the vault folder or your codebase root.
agentSetup.stepConnectionCheck: In Claude Code, run /mcp; in Codex, run codex mcp list to confirm the server is connected.
agentSetup.stepGate: Run the JSON gate and check ok separately from performanceOk before edits.
agentSetup.stepMcpVerify: Run mcp-verify from the agent root to prove the 23 local tools are reachable.
agentSetup.stepGraphProof: Read workspace-brief and agent-brief before the first ontology write.
agentSetup.proofVaultLoaded: {count} docs loaded from this local vault
agentSetup.proofHealthClean: vault validation has no errors or warnings
agentSetup.proofHealthUnknown: validation has not reported a result in this panel
agentSetup.proofConfigsReady: Claude Code / Cursor / Codex config files are ready
agentSetup.proofAgentRootReady: restart from the vault folder, or copy the codebase-root templates when working elsewhere
agentSetup.proofAgentRootNeedsTemplate: copy the setup packet before opening Claude Code or Codex from another codebase root
agentSetup.proofJsonGateManual: copy and run the JSON gate in the agent root before edits
agentSetup.proofContractConfigStateDesc: agent-setup --json checks root-specific Claude Code / Cursor and Codex config readiness before repair.
agentSetup.proofContractMcpVerifyDesc: mcp-verify boots the local MCP server, lists the 23 tools, and reads the target vault.
agentSetup.proofContractJsonGateDesc: agent-brief --verify-fallbacks --json reports ok and performanceOk before edits.
agentSetup.proofContractGraphBriefsDesc: workspace-brief and agent-brief --graph-db-pack describe the same local vault before writes.
agentSetup.invalidRepairHint: Existing config files are preserved. Copy the setup packet or codebase-root templates, then replace the reviewed file manually.
agentSetup.repairTitle: Creates only the missing .mcp.json, .codex/config.toml, and .mcp.json.example files. It does not add starter markdown files.
agentSetup.copyPromptTitle: Copy the read-first MCP prompt to verify Claude Code or Codex before edits.
agentSetup.openWorkflowGuideTitle: Open the Agent Graph Workflow guide explaining CLI-only use, MCP-connected use, graph DB differences, and actual verification commands.
agentSetup.copyPacketTitle: Copy one packet with MCP/Codex config templates, restart guidance, verification prompt, and CLI fallback.
agentSetup.copyCliTitle: Copy terminal commands for validation, agent handoff, fallback self-check, and graph traversal when the oh-my-ontology CLI is already installed.
agentSetup.copyFirstContactProofTitle: Copy the minimal setup gate, workspace-brief, agent-brief, graph DB pack, and mcp-verify proof sequence for a codebase-root agent session.
agentSetup.modeCliDesc: Run validation, summaries, graph scans, path/explain queries, and graph DB packs from Terminal without MCP.
agentSetup.modeMcpDesc: Claude Code, Codex, or Cursor can call 23 tools directly with structured repair fields and write guardrails.
agentSetup.modeGraphDesc: Explore with query plans, node/edge scans, domain matrix, paths, and relation explanations without a database server.
agentSetup.modeGateDesc: When setup is unclear or the agent opens at a codebase root, check JSON readiness and performanceOk before edits.
agentSetup.copyJsonGateTitle: Copy the timeout-bounded JSON self-check command that Claude Code / Codex setup automation can parse directly.
agentSetup.gateBrokenDesc: Setup or fallback execution is broken. Fix config before ontology edits.
agentSetup.gateSlowDesc: The local graph works, but fallback latency is slow enough to investigate.
agentSetup.gateReadyDesc: When ok and performanceOk are both true, read-first agent work can start.
agentSetup.syncAfterChangeDesc: When a non-trivial change introduces or renames a domain, capability, element, or relation, sync docs/ontology before finishing; skip typos, comments, style-only, lint-config, and fixture-only changes.
agentSetup.copyPostChangeSyncTitle: Copy the MCP and CLI post-change ontology sync gate for Claude Code, Codex, or teammate handoff.
agentSetup.cliPreviewAriaLabel: Preview of copied CLI graph runbook
agentSetup.rootVaultDesc: When this vault folder is the agent root, verify and graph brief commands use `.` as the vault.
agentSetup.rootCodebaseDesc: When the agent opens in another product codebase, setup state, repair, mcp-verify, and JSON gate commands must pass this vault's absolute path.
agentSetup.copySetupCheckCliTitle: Copy the read-first JSON command that checks missing and invalid setup state before connecting an existing vault from a separate codebase root.
agentSetup.copyTemplateTitle: Copy the .mcp.json template for agents opened at a separate codebase root. Replace the vault path placeholder before using it.
agentSetup.copySetupCliTitle: Copy the safe CLI command that connects an existing vault from a separate codebase root by creating only missing Claude Code / Cursor / Codex config files.
agentSetup.copyCodexTemplateTitle: Copy the .codex/config.toml template for Codex opened at a separate codebase root. Replace the vault path placeholder before using it.
agentSetup.copyCodexCliTitle: Copy a one-line codex mcp add command for registering this vault from any codebase root. Replace the vault path placeholder before running it.

topology.emptyBody: Auto-generate the Folder-Topology file structure (projects/_.md, categories.md, statuses.md) and the topology will show up. Existing files are not overwritten.
topology.scaffoldCta: Initialize this folder as a topology vault

commands.scaffoldTopology: Initialize this folder as a topology vault

dialog.deleteConfirm:
Really delete?

{title}
{slug}.md

This cannot be undone.
dialog.scaffoldConfirm:
Initialize this folder as a Folder-Topology vault?

- projects/ directory + 2 sample projects
- categories.md · statuses.md default sets
- README.md

Existing files are not overwritten.
dialog.scaffoldDone:
Initialization complete — {created} new files · {skipped} skipped

Open the freshly generated README.md first. The starter also writes .mcp.json and .codex/config.toml so Claude Code, Cursor, or Codex can attach to this vault folder.
dialog.ontologyStarterDone: Ontology starter created — {created} new files · {skipped} skipped. Opening README.md.
dialog.agentVerifyPromptCopyFailed: Could not copy agent verification prompt
dialog.noHeadings: This document has no h2/h3 headings.
dialog.notLocalFile: This document is not a local vault file.
dialog.vaultConflict: This vault file was modified externally (other editor / AI). Refresh and save again.
dialog.notRendered: Document is not rendered yet. Try again.
dialog.importOverwriteConfirm:
{count} documents already exist in the vault.

Yes = Overwrite (saveDoc)
No = Skip duplicates

Proceed?

topology.selectionAnnouncement: {name} selected. {deps, plural, one {# dependency} other {# dependencies}}, {referenced, plural, one {# reference} other {# references}}.

errorFallback.title: Topology failed to render
errorFallback.body: WebGL context may have been lost or there's a GPU resource issue. Try again or switch to the tree view.

workspace.subtitle: {concepts} concepts · {relations} relations{growth}
workspace.description: {relations} relations connect {concepts} ontology concepts. Click a node to open its details.

ontologyDrawer.caption: Selected node
ontologyDrawer.collaboratorBody: Use this as the shared vocabulary for planning, marketing, domain review, and agent handoff.
ontologyDrawer.collaboratorHandoffProfileStep: Inspect the selected concept profile before changing language or scope.
ontologyDrawer.collaboratorHandoffImpactStep: Trace incoming impact so dependents are visible before review.
ontologyDrawer.collaboratorHandoffSyncStep: After a real change, run the shared ontology sync gate.

collaboratorReview.defineOwner: Review: define the owner and the next useful relation before this concept becomes shared language.
collaboratorReview.explainUsage: Review: explain what this concept depends on and why those links matter to the work.
collaboratorReview.confirmDependents: Review: confirm who relies on this concept before renaming, deleting, or changing its meaning.
collaboratorReview.traceImpact: Review: trace impact in both directions before changing messaging, scope, or implementation.

collaboratorImpact.needsOwner: No direct graph relations yet. Define ownership and containment before using this as shared vocabulary.
collaboratorImpact.usageOnly: Outgoing relations explain what this node uses. Check those dependencies before changing wording.
collaboratorImpact.dependentOnly: Incoming relations show who depends on this node. Review dependents before changing scope or naming.
collaboratorImpact.bidirectional: This node has both dependents and dependencies. Trace both directions before changing messaging, scope, or implementation.

collaboratorReviewQuestions.defineOwnerRelation: Which domain, capability, or project should contain it?
collaboratorReviewQuestions.defineOwnerMeaning: What meaning should be true before others reuse the term?
collaboratorReviewQuestions.explainUsageDepends: What does this concept depend on?
collaboratorReviewQuestions.explainUsageWhy: Why does that dependency matter to the product or implementation?
collaboratorReviewQuestions.explainUsageAudience: Who needs this explanation before they can use the term correctly?
collaboratorReviewQuestions.confirmDependentsWho: Who or what currently relies on this concept?
collaboratorReviewQuestions.confirmDependentsChange: What breaks if the name, scope, or meaning changes?
collaboratorReviewQuestions.confirmDependentsNotify: Which owner or reviewer should confirm the change?
collaboratorReviewQuestions.traceImpactIncoming: Which incoming references should be checked first?
collaboratorReviewQuestions.traceImpactOutgoing: Which outgoing dependencies explain the current meaning?
collaboratorReviewQuestions.traceImpactBoundary: Which product, domain, or implementation boundary should not move?

createNode.toastExists: A node with that slug already exists.

explanationEdit.heading: Explanation

analysis.healthSyncGateCopiedAriaLabel: Topology health post-repair sync gate copied

controls.reviewAria: {count} changes since baseline — review in the ontology changes panel

empty.noMatchesBody: Search, depth, or hub filters are hiding every concept and project.
empty.sparseFilterBody: The current filters leave too few concepts or projects to draw a connected map.
empty.kicker: Relation map · {count, plural, one {# project} other {# projects}}
empty.bodyNoProjectsDownload: Install the macOS app and open a local vault to start the relation map from the first project concept.
empty.bodyNoProjectsPicker: Open a local vault or save the first project concept in Save/edit to start the relation map.
empty.bodyNoDeps: Save one relation between concepts and its line will appear here.
empty.crossViewHint: Full ontology node documents and changes stay available in Browse and Save/edit.

hint.title: Ontology terrain map
hint.body: Nodes are projects · domains · capabilities · elements. Lines are depends-on / related / contains. Click to open details, search to narrow.

ontologyView.title: Ontology workbench

titleTooltip.heading: Local markdown graph workbench
titleTooltip.body: An ontology graph grown directly from the frontmatter of `.md` files in your vault. The tree is the browse view for scanning <hierarchy>project → domain → capability → element</hierarchy> hierarchy.
titleTooltip.footer: Document nodes act as evidence and are excluded from the tree. Use Save/edit for writes and Insights for graph DB-style scans.

ontologyView.titleTooltipAria: Explain what the ontology workbench is

actions.workbenchOverviewTooltip: Show the Browse · Write · Query flow
actions.globalSearchAria: Open global search across concepts and projects
actions.queryTooltip: Insights — validate scans, matrix, and path evidence through the graph DB pack
actions.queryAria: Ontology insights — open the graph DB query pack
actions.builderTooltip: Open Save/edit — place concepts, review relations, and write frontmatter changes.
actions.builderAria: Open Save/edit for concept and relation changes
actions.primeAgentTooltip: Copy one complete briefing — mental model, readiness, MCP query order, traversal + write guardrails, entry points, CLI fallback. Paste once into Claude Code / Codex / Cursor.
actions.primeAgentAria: Copy the complete agent onboarding briefing to the clipboard
actions.primeAgentCopied: Agent briefing copied — readiness {status} ({score}/100). Paste into Claude Code / Codex / Cursor.
actions.primeAgentCopyError: Couldn't copy the briefing. Try again.

agentStatus.triggerAria: Open MCP setup status for AI agents — {status}, readiness {score}
agentStatus.title: Let Claude Code · Codex read the same vault
agentStatus.body: When MCP is connected, the agent can query {relations} relations directly. Review {blockers} blockers in connection details first.
agentStatus.connectionMode: Claude Code checks MCP servers through .mcp.json or /mcp; Codex connects through .codex/config.toml or codex mcp add/list.
agentStatus.footnote: The app does not open Claude Code or Codex chat inside Context Atlas. The briefing and setup check let each agent read this local markdown graph from its own app or terminal through MCP or the CLI fallback path.

changes.emptyHint: Mark a baseline to track what changes from now — added, edited, and removed concepts appear here as you and your AI agent edit the vault. Ideal for reviewing a session in a meeting.
changes.scopedHint: Tree scoped to {count} changed concepts (and their parents)
changes.copyAgentAria: Copy ontology changes for Claude Code or Codex
changes.markReviewed: Mark “{title}” reviewed (drops from the changeset; re-flags if it changes again)
changes.impact: {count} concepts depend on this — review its impact (blast radius)

stat.ariaLabel: Ontology tree role and source status
stat.roleHint: Browse project → domain → capability → element. Write in Save/edit; query in Insights.
stat.graphRefsHint: Screen counts are simplified for reading. They come from vault frontmatter, not a separate database.
stat.evidenceHint: Document nodes stay as evidence so the tree remains a concept map.
stat.warningsHint: Some valid graph relations cannot be shown as one hierarchy.

treeWarnings.title: {count} relations outside the hierarchy
treeWarnings.body: The graph is still queryable. These warnings mean the hierarchy view kept one parent or promoted a cycle so the tree stays readable. Use Insights for graph DB-style scans and Save/edit to review relation direction before changing frontmatter.
treeWarnings.dialogDescription: Summary keeps the workbench readable; raw notes are available when an agent or maintainer needs exact graph evidence.
treeWarnings.rawHint: Raw notes are emitted by the deterministic tree projection. They are evidence for graph review, not a separate approval queue.

multiple-parent.label: Multiple parents
multiple-parent.hint: The tree shows the first parent only. Use Insights scan for the full relation set.

cycle.label: Cycle
cycle.hint: Cycle nodes are promoted to roots. Review relation direction in Save/edit.

self-parent.label: Self parent
self-parent.hint: Edges where a node parents itself are ignored by the tree.

duplicate.label: Duplicate reach
duplicate.hint: If one node is reached twice, the second tree position is skipped.

other.label: Other projection note
other.hint: Graph state outside the hierarchy projection.

workbench.activeSlugBody: Write and Query keep the canonical slug selected in Browse.
workbench.treeBody: Scan hierarchy, select a node, and inspect reachability, ego graph, and agent handoff context.
workbench.treeLoopAction: Start by choosing the concept slug that the next action should keep in focus.
workbench.builderBody: Organize {count} containment relations on canvas and save new nodes or relations to the same vault frontmatter.
workbench.builderLoopAction: Continue by opening the same slug in the canvas before writing frontmatter.
workbench.builderAriaLabel: Write relations in the concept save/edit canvas
workbench.graphDbBody: Validate node scans, edge scans, domain matrix, containment traversal, and path evidence through the graph DB pack.
workbench.graphDbLoopAction: Close the loop by proving scans, containment, paths, and impact against the local markdown graph.
workbench.graphDbAriaLabel: Open Graph DB query pack insights

ontologyView.error: Something went wrong while loading ontology data. {message}
ontologyView.emptyHint: No concepts are visible yet. Add a `.md` file with `kind` frontmatter in a local vault to show concepts and relations.

getStarted.headingDefault: Open a local vault to start the concept map
getStarted.bodyLocal: The active vault has no `.md` file with `kind` frontmatter. Add frontmatter to one document or create your first concept in Save/edit.
getStarted.bodyDefault: Context Atlas reads document frontmatter into a local graph of concepts and relations. Open a local vault first, then add your first concept.
getStarted.stepLocalFrontmatterDesc: Add frontmatter like `kind: capability` to any `.md` file in the vault and a stub concept appears immediately.
getStarted.stepLocalBuilderDesc: Use the /ontology/edit canvas to visually polish concepts and relations — saves to the vault and updates the tree.
getStarted.stepStaticVaultDescDownload: The hosted browser is read-only. Install the macOS app, then pick a local vault folder there.
getStarted.stepStaticVaultDescPicker: Open a local vault folder to activate writable local work.
getStarted.stepStaticFrontmatterDesc: Write kind / capabilities / elements / relates in your docs and stub concepts are created automatically.
getStarted.stepStaticBuilderDesc: Polish concepts and relations on the /ontology/edit canvas and they grow right into this tree.
getStarted.snippetSummary: Your vault's first concept — paste this frontmatter into a `.md` file and it appears in the tree right away
getStarted.snippetHelp: Save the content above into your vault as something like capabilities/auth.md. More frontmatter keys: domain · capabilities · elements · relates · dependencies.

footer.countsHint: Includes implicit stubs and the tree projection — may differ from the number of .md files or graph DB proof compile counts.

copyLink.tooltip: Copy a direct link to this concept

detail.reviewCopySyncGateTitle: Copy the post-change ontology sync gate for Claude Code, Codex, or teammate handoff.

reviewImpact.needs_owner: No direct graph relations yet. Define owner and container before this becomes shared vocabulary.
reviewImpact.usage_only: Outgoing relations explain what this concept uses. Check those dependencies before changing wording.
reviewImpact.dependent_only: Incoming relations show who depends on this concept. Review dependents before changing scope or naming.
reviewImpact.bidirectional: This concept has both dependents and dependencies. Trace both directions before changing messaging, scope, or implementation.

detail.reviewRelationPreviewDeck: Check this node's frontmatter edges first, then continue into topology, save/edit, or query.

reviewPrompt.define_owner: Define the owner, audience, and the plain-language meaning before adding more relations.
reviewPrompt.explain_usage: Explain what this concept uses or depends on, then check whether the dependency names match team vocabulary.
reviewPrompt.confirm_dependents: Confirm who depends on this concept before renaming, merging, or removing it.
reviewPrompt.trace_impact: Trace both incoming and outgoing impact before changing the concept or its vocabulary.

reviewQuestions.defineOwnerContainer: Which domain, capability, or project should contain it?
reviewQuestions.defineOwnerMeaning: What plain-language meaning should others reuse?
reviewQuestions.explainUsageDepends: What does this concept use or depend on?
reviewQuestions.explainUsageWhy: Why does that dependency matter to the product or implementation?
reviewQuestions.explainUsageAudience: Who needs this explanation before reusing the term?
reviewQuestions.confirmDependentsWho: Who or what currently depends on this concept?
reviewQuestions.confirmDependentsChange: What breaks if the name, scope, or meaning changes?
reviewQuestions.confirmDependentsReviewer: Which owner or reviewer should confirm the change?
reviewQuestions.traceImpactIncoming: Which incoming references should be checked first?
reviewQuestions.traceImpactOutgoing: Which outgoing dependencies explain the current meaning?
reviewQuestions.traceImpactBoundary: Which product, domain, or implementation boundary should not move?

detail.agentContextCopyToastError: Could not copy node profile command.
detail.reachabilityEmpty: No nodes are reachable with the current direction and depth.
detail.reachabilityLimited: Limited to the closest reachable nodes.
detail.egoTwoHopTooltip: Includes neighbors one step further — labels may overlap with more nodes
detail.neighborMissingTitle: A node with this ID isn't in the graph yet
detail.stubWarning: Unresolved reference — the slug pointed to by frontmatter doesn't exist in the vault. Create a node with this name in Save/edit (/ontology/edit), or remove the reference from frontmatter.

localVaultPicker.unsupported: Local vault editing is unavailable here. Install the macOS app to open and edit vault folders on disk.
localVaultPicker.permissionNeeded: We have a previously opened folder. Re-authorize to keep using it.
localVaultPicker.errorFallback: Local vault error — there was a problem accessing the folder.
localVaultPicker.errorHint: Re-check folder permission or try a different folder.
localVaultPicker.idleHint: Pick a local folder with markdown files. Frontmatter becomes the graph, and nothing is uploaded.
localVaultPicker.validationTooltip: {errors, plural, =0 {{warnings} frontmatter warning(s) — run pnpm vault:validate for detail} other {{errors} frontmatter error(s){warnings, plural, =0 {} other {, {warnings} warning(s)}} — some nodes are missing from the graph. Run pnpm vault:validate for detail}}

starterCta.emptyBodyLine1: Creates 5 starter markdown files (project / domain / capability / element + README), .mcp.json, and .codex/config.toml in this folder.
starterCta.emptyBodyLine2: Edit the frontmatter to match your project and it shows up in the tree, topology, and Save/edit right away.
starterCta.definitionBody: A codebase ontology is an executable meaning model: projects, domains, capabilities, elements, and typed relations that explain ownership, dependency, evidence, and impact.
starterCta.proofLocalBody: Markdown in the folder is the database. No account or server; it reads and writes your PC files.
starterCta.proofGraphBody: workspace_brief and agent_brief verify nodes, relations, health, and handoff order.
starterCta.proofAgentBody: Claude Code, Cursor, and Codex check the same vault read-first before editing.
starterCta.verifyAriaLabel: Starter includes AI agent verification steps
starterCta.verifyStepFiles: Agent configs are created for Claude Code, Cursor, and Codex.
starterCta.verifyStepMcp: The README asks the agent to run validate_vault, workspace_brief, and agent_brief before edits.
starterCta.verifyStepCli: CLI users get matching fallback self-check and mcp-verify commands for terminal proof.
starterCta.secondaryTitle: Adds 5 ontology starter files plus agent verification guidance (existing files are left untouched)
starterCta.secondaryCopyTitle: Copy the read-first MCP verification prompt without adding starter files
starterCta.secondaryCliTitle: Copy commands that prove the same connection in a terminal without an agent
starterCta.secondaryJsonGateTitle: Copy the JSON self-check command that Claude Code / Codex setup automation can parse directly

vaultStubs.emptyBody: No `.md` file in the local vault has `kind:` frontmatter yet. Add `kind: project` to the top of a document and a concept appears immediately.
vaultStubs.intro: Summary of concepts and relations read from the current local vault. Browse in the tree below; write relations in Save/edit.
vaultStubs.polishSummary: Want to polish concepts and relations visually?
vaultStubs.polishBody: Open the same local vault in the `/ontology/edit/` save/edit canvas to adjust concept positions and frontmatter keys inline. Saves go back to the same `.md` files.

sections.basicsDescription: Settle the name, slug, category, and status.
sections.basicsDetailedDescription: First align the name, identifier, and current category and status.
sections.storyDescription: Enter description, markdown body, tags, and links.
sections.storyDetailedDescription: Fill the description shown on node cards, drawers, and the detail page.
sections.networkDetailedDescription: Review dependencies and screen materials together.
sections.operationsDescription: Wrap up timeline, owner, icon, and hub flag.
sections.operationsDetailedDescription: Finish operational signals like timeline, owner, and hub flag.

fields.name: Name
fields.slugHint: Used in URLs. Letters, digits, and hyphens only. Cannot change after creation.
fields.duplicateNotice: Starting from a copy of the existing project.
fields.categoryMissingWarning: Current category reference is broken. Pick another category to recover.
fields.statusMissingWarning: Current status reference is broken. Pick another status to recover.
fields.descriptionPlaceholder: 1-2 line summary — shown in drawer and node
fields.detailPlaceholder:
# Heading

**Markdown supported** — shown on detail page
fields.dependenciesHint: Pick the hubs or upstream projects this one connects to.

validation.globalErrorBanner: There are items to fix before saving.
validation.globalErrorMore: + {count} more. Check each in the fields below.

actions.headerHelpCreate: Save first; you can fill the rest later.
actions.createNoticeStay: Project created — ready to keep editing.
actions.editNoticeStay: Saved the changes. You can verify on the right preview and the public link.

tip.lead: Just enter name, status, and a short description.
tip.step1: 1. Entering a name auto-generates the slug.
tip.step3: 3. Even a single-line description shows up in cards and drawers.
tip.step4: 4. Add connections and ops info later by reopening.

preview.liveHint: Inputs on the left reflect here and on public screens immediately.
preview.noChanges: Same as saved. Move on to compare against the public screen.
preview.cardCaption: How it actually renders in the topology.
preview.changeCategoryStatus: Category and status will look like "{category} / {status}".
preview.changeDescriptionFilled: Short description will reflect on cards and drawers immediately.
preview.changeDescriptionEmpty: Short description is empty, so card and drawer summaries shrink.
preview.changeDetailFilled: Detail body will be shown on the public detail page.
preview.changeDetailEmpty: Detail body is empty, so the public detail page is simplified.

dependencyPicker.emptyHint: No dependencies selected. Pick from below to add.
dependencyPicker.suggestionsHeading: Suggestion — other projects found in the description
dependencyPicker.missingHint: These project references no longer exist. Click chips to remove them one by one.

quickCreate.fieldDescriptionPlaceholder: Describe what this project does in one line.
quickCreate.errorRequired: Please enter a project name and one-line description first.

quickEdit.headerSubtitle: Change name and description without breaking your reading flow.
quickEdit.fieldDescriptionPlaceholder: Describe this project in one line…
quickEdit.errorEmpty: Name and one-line description cannot be empty.

publicQuickActions.infoHintBody: Attach docs that explain this project, or edit project info in detail.
publicQuickActions.infoNewBody: Add a new project to the workspace you are in.
publicQuickActions.infoEditBody: Edit project details — status, tags, links, and more.
